evidence: We have implemented an AI Governance and Enablement Program to oversee the use of AI in our business.

application of artificial intelligence and machine learning to battlefield decisions... develop, test, and integrate leading-edge AI and machine learning algorithms to optimize and accelerate the nation's mission-critical systems
application of artificial intelligence and machine learning to battlefield decisions
develop, test, and integrate leading-edge AI and machine learning algorithms to optimize and accelerate the nation's mission-critical systems and platforms
utilize artificial intelligence, including generative artificial intelligence, machine learning... to enhance productivity and operational efficiency and optimize processes
If the AI tools that we use are deficient, incomplete, inaccurate, biased... we could incur operational inefficiencies, competitive harm, legal liability, brand or reputational harm
degraded or flawed performance of the AI tools... may result from adversarial attacks that include data poisoning, malware risks, and evasion techniques
AI regulation is rapidly evolving worldwide... We may not be able to anticipate how to respond to these rapidly evolving frameworks

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified dollar amounts, headcount, or capex figures tied to AI investment are disclosed in the excerpts; named program (AI Governance and Enablement Program) and named use cases justify score 3 but not higher.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Use cases are named and described as deployed/operational (battlefield decisions, mission-critical systems, productivity enhancement), but no quantified business results are provided in the excerpts.
capex_mentions: No dollar or quantity figures tied to AI/compute investment appear in the excerpts.
